headlineSummary: Google Gemini drew the harshest reaction around the CLI shutdown and product-strategy criticism, Anthropic was negative on limited evidence, and OpenAI stayed mixed while closely tied with Microsoft Copilot, which was mostly neutral on limited evidence [E7][E8][E1][E16][E17].

ranking.summary: Gemini was the most negative name in the discussion, driven by the CLI shutdown and repeated criticism of Google’s product decisions, with only a small positive note in the mix [E7][E8][E11][E13].
Anthropic was negative on limited evidence, mainly from complaints about Claude Code usage limits and a comparison that placed Opus behind another model [E5][E6].

OpenAI landed in mixed territory: one thread praised a model’s math result and another praised Codex, but there was also distrust and a strong complaint about content exploitation; it was in a close tie with Microsoft Copilot [E1][E2][E4][E14][E16].

Microsoft Copilot was mostly neutral on limited evidence, with the cited discussion simply noting Copilot-related dashboard files rather than offering a clear opinion; it was in a close tie with OpenAI [E17].

evidence.excerpt: An OpenAI model has disproved a central conjecture in discrete geometry

annotations.rationale: The title attributes a major mathematical result to an OpenAI model, conveying a favorable achievement.

evidence.excerpt: I must say that this is rather exciting.

annotations.rationale: In the OpenAI story context, the commenter explicitly calls the result exciting.

evidence.excerpt: I like how everyone laughed when OpenAI said their models will have "PhD-Level Intelligence" and now the goalpost has been moved to if AI can create new math

annotations.rationale: The commenter defends OpenAI's earlier capability claim in light of this result.

evidence.excerpt: although I honestly wouldn't put that past OpenAI.

annotations.rationale: This expresses distrust toward OpenAI regarding possible undisclosed training-data practices.

evidence.excerpt: better than Opus 4.7, Gemini 3.1 Pro and GPT5.5!

annotations.rationale: Saying Qwen is better than Opus 4.7 implies Anthropic's compared model is inferior here.
Saying Qwen is better than Gemini 3.1 Pro implies Gemini underperforms in this comparison.
Saying Qwen is better than GPT5.5 implies the compared OpenAI model is weaker on this metric.

evidence.excerpt: I was getting dangerously close to my weekly Claude Code limit last night

annotations.rationale: The commenter complains about hitting Claude Code usage limits.

evidence.excerpt: Gemini CLI will stop working from June 18, 2026

annotations.rationale: A product ceasing to work is negative access/release news for Gemini CLI.

evidence.excerpt: Google really can’t help themselves but to have some internal re-org kill off a public thing people are actively using. It’s honestly impressive how consistent they are.

annotations.rationale: In context of Gemini CLI's shutdown, this is a strong criticism of Google's product management.

evidence.excerpt: developers will never adopt our products if we keep killing them

annotations.rationale: The commenter argues repeated shutdowns will prevent developer adoption of Google's products, strongly criticizing the strategy behind Gemini CLI's replacement.

evidence.excerpt: Gemini Cli was already losing and it’s now being replaced by something they’re saying doesn’t yet have feature parity. Doesn’t seem likely to inspire defections from competitors.

annotations.rationale: The commenter says Gemini CLI was already behind and the replacement is not competitive.

evidence.excerpt: all the Gemini CLI and Antigravity needs to be one , because some other manager thought Antigravity was a better name than Gemini or whatever and started this mess in the first place.

annotations.rationale: This blames poor internal decision-making for the Gemini CLI/Antigravity transition.

evidence.excerpt: Genuinely can't tell wtf google even wants me to use. Vertex? Gemini? Antigravity? Antigravity 2? Agent platform? Google One? Gemini Enterprise? Google AI?

annotations.rationale: The commenter strongly criticizes Google's confusing product lineup including Gemini.

evidence.excerpt: I'm not saying it's perfect, but I enjoy using it.

annotations.rationale: In context, the commenter expresses mild approval of using Gemini CLI.

evidence.excerpt: I'm good, I'll stick with pi and codex. Less is more my friends.

annotations.rationale: The commenter prefers Codex over Google's replacement CLI, indicating mild positive sentiment toward OpenAI's tool.

evidence.excerpt: It seems like they use Gemini 3.1 Pro to run their production decisions.

annotations.rationale: This is a sarcastic jab implying Gemini 3.1 Pro would make poor operational decisions.

evidence.excerpt: If I put in time and effort to make content and OpenAI et al copy it and sell it through their LLM such that no one comes to me any more, then plainly it makes no sense for me to create that content

annotations.rationale: The commenter accuses OpenAI of exploiting creators' content for profit, expressing strong hostility.

evidence.excerpt: 680:-rw-r--r-- 1 root root 306146 May 18 23:14 copilot-abuse-dashboard.tar.gz 681:-rw-r--r-- 1 root root 219637 May 18 23:03 copilot-abuse.tar.gz

annotations.rationale: The leaked GitHub repo names appear to reference GitHub Copilot abuse-monitoring, but the excerpt is descriptive rather than evaluative.
